authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines defense lifecycle governance and escalation authority boundaries for mission systems workflows before service support decisions can advance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service triage and authority boundary decisions

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.91, Product Support Management for the Adaptive Acquisition Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides support-oriented obligations and release-readiness expectations relevant to mission systems service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Readiness state transitions and outage dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 4140.01, DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Gives mission-relevant material-availability and provisioning context that changes support timing and cross-team routing in service scenarios.
used_for: service delay and resource constraint interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Revision 2, Computer Security Incident Handling
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines incident support stages and evidence expectations for handoff and escalation in mission support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident triage and containment decision support

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171, Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets control requirements for handling sensitive mission data during support and change-ready workflows.
used_for: refusal when authority is not verified

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1, Information Technology Service Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides recognized service-management structure for support records, closure conditions, and process handoff quality.

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether the request is within mission-system support scope and whether required tenants and artifact gates are present before action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Troubleshooting and resolution preparation
workflow_stages.description: Classify incident or service state and map required evidence, dependency, and approval blockers before proposing next action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and communication
workflow_stages.description: Assemble bounded resolution artifacts for supported cases and capture what remains unresolved for transparent handoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and refusal management
workflow_stages.description: Escalate low-confidence, unsupported exception, policy-conflict, and authority-gap cases instead of forced completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff and change-readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Prepare cross-team routing notes and change-readiness escalation when integration dependencies or approvals are incomplete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Change requests, change logs, and rollback evidence
Test results and validation check artifacts
systems_of_record: Mission operations and interface monitoring systems

allowed: Validate scope, evidence sufficiency, and dependency risks before recommending service or support progression.
Prepare escalation-ready incident summaries with owner and impact when blockers remain.
Route legal, safety, security, engineering, and financial-approval asks to adjacent authorities.
escalate: Critical dependency or ownership ambiguity across teams
refuse: Unsupported policy exceptions or workaround requests
Requests for final safety/legal/financial or mission authority decisions
Change progression without test, rollback, and approver evidence

domain_failure_modes: Treating mission-specific terms as generic support language and skipping tenant-specific taxonomy checks.
Completing support actions without validated access, rollback, or approver evidence.
Overriding interface or dependency blockers due to urgency pressure.
Routing to wrong adjacent owner because authority map is not validated in request context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
System dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
Requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant definitions for mission-readiness, interface lock, or change lock differ across commands and platforms.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat the tenant-specific definitions as mandatory inputs and request explicit mapping before completion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policy on security and release evidence
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant approval depth for access and mission release artifacts can materially change safe disposition.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy references at runtime and escalate unresolved cases instead of guessing.

notes: Research source set uses primary defense governance plus recognized service-management and incident-control references.
Workflow, evidence, terminology, and refusal boundaries are covered through service, support, and authority stages.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because all key differences are converted into required retrieval and escalation conditions.
